zh.lifeSummary: 當Joan Corbet於大約1332年出生在英國英格蘭史洛普爾莫雷頓 可特時，她的父親Robert Corbet29歲，母親Elizabeth Le Strange25歲。她於1356年與Robert De Harley在英國英格蘭史洛普爾莫雷頓 可特結婚。他們至少是1個女兒的父母。她去世於1417年6月20日之後在威爾士蒙格莫利夏爾麗頓。

nameMeanings.name: Corbet
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: History: Walter Corbet, a royal knight in 12th-century Scotland, was the first of that name in that kingdom. His 13th-century descendants were major landholders in the Borders, and in the 14th century they acquired lands in the North of Scotland.

variants.name: Corbett
meanings: English (West Midlands, of Norman origin): nickname from Old French corbet ‘raven’, probably denoting someone with dark hair or a dark complexion.

History: This is the name of a family descended from Hugh Corbet, a Norman baron who settled in Shropshire following the Norman Conquest. One of his descendants, Sir Richard Corbet, was granted land near Shrewsbury in 1223; since the 13th century, this place has been known as Moreton Corbet. The name was taken from Shropshire to Scotland in the 12th century and to northern Ireland in the 17th century, and thence to North America by at least one group of bearers of the name.

nameMeanings.name: Joan
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Contracted form of Old French Jo(h)anne, from Latin Io(h)anna ( see Joanna ). In England this was the usual feminine form of John from the Middle English period onwards and was extremely popular, but in the 16th and 17th centuries it steadily lost ground to Jane . It was strongly revived in the first part of the 20th century, partly under the influence of George Bernard Shaw's play St Joan ( 1923 ), based on the life of Joan of Arc ( 1412–31 ). Claiming to be guided by the voices of the saints, she persuaded the French dauphin to defy the occupying English forces and have himself crowned, and she led the French army that raised the siege of Orleans in 1429 . The following year she was captured by the Burgundians and sold to the English, and a year later she was burned at the stake for witchcraft at the age of 18 or 19. Her story has captured the imagination of many writers, and she is variously portrayed as a national and political hero, a model of apolitical straightforwardness and honesty, and a religious heroine. She was canonized in 1920 . More recent influences have included the American film actress Joan Crawford ( 1908–77 , born Lucille le Sueur ), the British actress Joan Collins ( b. 1933 ), the American comedienne Joan Rivers ( b. 1933 ), and the West Indian pop singer Joan Armatrading ( b. 1950 ).

variants.name: John
meanings: form of   John  , also used quite frequently in the English-speaking world. In Northern Ireland it is sometimes found as a variant of   Ewan .

en.lifeSummary: When Joan Corbet was born about 1332, in Moreton Corbet, Shropshire, England, United Kingdom, her father, Robert Corbet, was 29 and her mother, Elizabeth Le Strange, was 25. She married Robert De Harley in 1356, in Moreton Corbet, Shropshire, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 1 daughter. She died after 20 June 1417, in Leighton, Montgomeryshire, Wales.

ko.lifeSummary: Joan Corbet(이)가 대략 1332년 영국 잉글랜드 슈럽실르 모레턴 코벳에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Robert Corbet(은)는 29세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Elizabeth Le Strange(은)는 25세였다. 그녀는 1356년 영국 잉글랜드 슈럽실르 모레턴 코벳에서 Robert De Harley(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1417년 6월 20일 이후 웨일스 몽고머이실르 레이턴에서 사망했다.
